config._comment_strategy: Endpoints are MERGE-ed (not MATCH-ed) so an edge that arrives before its node events still lands: a bare CPGNode is created and later enriched by the node connector's MERGE on the same node_id. The relationship is MERGE-ed on the deterministic edge_id, so replaying the same edge never duplicates it. coalesce() accepts BOTH the Task 2 field names actually on the wire (source_id/target_id) and the Task 3 TOPIC_CONTRACT aliases (source_node_id/target_node_id). edge_type is kept as a property on a single CPG_EDGE relationship type (Cypher cannot parameterize the relationship type without APOC).
config.neo4j.cypher.topic.cpg.edges: MERGE (src:CPGNode {node_id: coalesce(event.source_id, event.source_node_id)}) MERGE (dst:CPGNode {node_id: coalesce(event.target_id, event.target_node_id)}) MERGE (src)-[r:CPG_EDGE {edge_id: event.edge_id}]->(dst) SET r.edge_type = event.edge_type, r.file_id = event.file_id, r.schema_version = toString(event.schema_version), r.event_time = event.event_time, r.properties_json = apoc.convert.toJson(event.properties)
